title: Revoke a long-term access token
content:
Use this endpoint to revoke a long-term access token. This is a new endpoint in TikTok API for Business v1.3. A long-term access token is a type of token that you can obtain after receiving authorization from an ad account, a Business Center account, or a TikTok One Creator Marketplace account. The token does not expire. ## Request **Endpoint** https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/oauth2/revoke_token/ **Method** POST **Header** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| ``` **Parameters** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |app_id{Required}|string|ID of your developer app.

To find the App ID, navigate to [My Apps](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/apps/) > App Detail > Basic Information.| |secret{Required}|string|Secret of your developer app.

To find the Secret, navigate to [My Apps](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/apps/) > App Detail > Basic Information. | |access_token{Required}|string|The long-term access token that you want to revoke.

You can create a new access token via [/oauth2/access_token/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1739965703387137#item-link-/oauth2/access_token/) or [/oauth/token/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1739965703387137#item-link-/oauth/token/).| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code curl http) curl --location --request POST 'https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/oauth2/revoke_token/' \ --header 'Access-Token: {{access_token}}' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "app_id": "{{app_id}}", "secret": "{{secret}}", "access_token": "{{access_token}}" }' (/code) ``` ## Response ```xtable |Field|Type |Description| |---|---|---| |code |number|Return code, see [Return Code](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097) | |message |string|Returned messages, see [Return information](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097) | |request_id |string| The log ID of a request, which uniquely identifies the request.| |data |object|Return data. | #|app_id |string|ID of your developer app.| #|advertiser_ids |string[]| The ID list of ad accounts that the token can no longer access.| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "{{request_id}}", "data": { "advertiser_ids": [ "{{advertiser_ids}}", "{{advertiser_ids}}" ], "app_id": "{{app_id}}" } } (/code); ```
